ideas.title: Turn one local-business lead into a campaign
ideas.about: Show a calm, ethical local-business version of the OpenClaw prospecting wave: find one public lead, identify a real marketing gap, mock up a useful offer, and draft a human outreach message. Keep it positioned as a workflow demo, not a get-rich-quick side hustle.
ideas.evidence: Full competitor scan surfaced Marcin's May 12 OpenClaw + Google Maps contractor outreach post at **15.3K views** with complete hook layers: on-screen title **"Openclaw + Google + Ai = $$$$ / Easy $10K a month"** and spoken hook **"Openclaw plus a clever guy using construction to make serious cash..."**. Riley's older OpenClaw Blender demo is still the largest competitor item returned at **699.9K views**. Braydon's own local-business and visual-output demos are strong: Claude UGC is **159.2K**, Arcads/product-link ad is **18.9K**, and Claude website build is **5.5K**.
ideas.onScreenTitle: AI can find local leads for you
ideas.spokenHook: If you run a local service business, this is how AI can help you find one real lead and turn it into a useful campaign idea without spamming people.
ideas.why: It borrows the OpenClaw wave but makes it Braydon: useful, ethical, local-business-first, and easy for Frank to understand.

ideas.title: Connect Claude to a visual tool
ideas.about: Reframe Claude as the front door for visual creation: give it a product photo, brand context, or campaign goal, then connect it to a visual tool to generate the asset. Use a simple before-and-after demo.
ideas.evidence: Justyn's May 12 Higgsfield MCP + Claude post is at **48.3K views**, **2,166 likes**, **629 shares**, with spoken hook **"Powerful as Claude is, there's one thing it just can't do until now and that's create high quality images and videos."** Braydon's own closest adjacent proof is huge: Claude UGC is **159.2K / 10,169 saves**, Blotato social content is **60.0K**, and Arcads from product link is **18.9K**.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Claude can now make the visual too
ideas.spokenHook: Claude is great at thinking through the campaign, but now you can connect it to tools that actually create the visual asset. Here is the simple version.
ideas.why: The audience has already proven they want Claude plus output demos. This gives the same pattern a fresh tool connection and a clear creative payoff.

ideas.title: The one context file your AI needs
ideas.about: Teach the simplest version of a context file: one page that tells AI who you are, what you do, your tone, your offers, your customers, and what good output looks like. Avoid dev language and call it a business briefing sheet.
ideas.evidence: Taki has two current Claude context/memory posts in the scan: **17.6K views / 226 comments** on a Claude handoff skill with on-screen title **"The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill"**, and **17.1K views / 234 shares** on a context file with on-screen title **"This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately"**. Both had spoken hooks extracted. Braydon's own skills/context-adjacent posts are lower-view but save-healthy, so this is a useful secondary, not the lock.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Give AI this one briefing sheet
ideas.spokenHook: Most people blame AI when the output is bad, but they never gave it the basics. Here is the one-page briefing sheet I would give every business owner.
ideas.why: It turns a technical Claude.md/context-file trend into a plain-English business habit. It also reinforces Braydon's core belief that AI is leadership and communication.

ideas.title: What 300 AI agents actually means
ideas.about: Use the Kimi K2.6 / 300 parallel agents headline as a teachable moment. Explain that the point is not "300 robots replacing people". The point is breaking one big job into smaller jobs that can run at the same time.
ideas.evidence: NoCode Joshua's Kimi post returned at **49.0K views** with on-screen title **"China just destroyed every AI 🤯"** and spoken hook **"China just did it again and dropped an AI model that competes with ChatGPT and Claude..."**. This is Frank-borderline if handled as model drama, but strong if Braydon turns it into a delegation lesson.
ideas.onScreenTitle: 300 AI agents sounds crazy. Here is what it means.
ideas.spokenHook: You do not need 300 AI agents. But this headline points to something every business owner should understand: AI work is moving from one chat to a team of smaller tasks.
ideas.why: It rides a competitor wave without copying the hype. Braydon can be the calm mentor who translates the trend into a practical leadership lesson.

ideas.title: Codex from your phone
ideas.about: OpenAI put Codex into the ChatGPT mobile app. Braydon's version should not be developer jargon. Make it about starting, checking, steering, and approving AI work while away from the desk.
ideas.evidence: Today's early-catch log still has strong official X signal from May 14: OpenAI's Codex mobile post at **20,519 likes**, **4,425 bookmarks**, and **2,135 quotes**. Braydon's own adjacent Claude Code + Codex post is at **12.4K views** with spoken hook **"You can now use Claude code and Codex together. Watch this."**
ideas.onScreenTitle: Codex can now work from your phone
ideas.spokenHook: Most people think Codex only matters at a computer, but OpenAI just put it in the ChatGPT mobile app. That means you can check the work and steer it from your phone.
ideas.why: It combines a fresh official product signal with Braydon's active Codex momentum. Keep the demo simple and outcome-based.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "PROMPT" and I'll send you hundreds of ChatGPT prompts 👇 ChatGPT agrees with everything you say by default. This 1 custom instruction changes everything. Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste this: "Prioritize critical thinking by identifying flaws, bias, or missing data in everything I say. Always offer the unspoken truth to make me think sharper." Now ChatGPT challenges you instead of flattering you. Have you ever noticed ChatGPT telling you what you want to hear? What would happen if your AI pushed back on your ideas? Want ChatGPT to make you smarter instead of agreeing with you? #chatgptprompts #chatgpt #ai #productivity #aitools

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: 80% of companies laying off employees because of AI saw zero financial benefit. Gartner's data backs this up. Most of those "AI layoff" announcements weren't about AI at all. They over-hired when money was cheap and interest rates were low. Blaming AI sounds smarter than admitting you missed revenue targets. The companies winning with AI aren't using it to replace people. They're using it as a redistribution layer. Support teams handle fewer tickets, but each interaction carries more weight because the easy ones are already automated. It's the right mindset. It's what I teach. It's how I run my own startup. Are AI layoffs real or PR spin? How do companies win with AI? What's the difference between AI replacement and AI redistribution? #ai #business #startup #entrepreneur #technology

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "REVEAL" and I'll send you all the prompts 👇 I asked ChatGPT to reveal everything it knows about me. The results were humbling. Here are the 4 prompts: 1. Based on everything you know about me, tell me who you think I'm pretending to be versus my true self. 2. Be brutally honest and tell me my deepest insecurities. 3. What is the 1 cycle I'm stuck in keeping me average forever? 4. If I fix this, what does the most successful version of my life look like in 5 years? ChatGPT paints 2 wildly different pictures of what your life looks like. What did ChatGPT say about you? Have you ever asked AI to be brutally honest with you? What's the 1 thing holding you back from your best life? #chatgpt #ai #chatgptprompts #psychology #aitools

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: 4 AI skills separating winners from losers in the AI gold rush. It's not about which tools you use. It's not about keeping up with AI news. It's 4 mental skills most people skip. 1. Change your default reaction. When you're stuck or confused, ask AI first. Before Google. Before asking someone else. 2. Treat AI like a new hire. Don't expect it to know everything on day 1. Give it feedback over time. 3. Continuously improve your AI setup. Ask AI to reflect on your conversation and update its own skills. Your CLAUDE.md should get better every single day. 4. Stop trying to replace entire roles. Use AI to get informed. So when you sit down with your accountant, you're asking better questions. How to use AI productively? AI skills for beginners? How to stop chasing AI tools? #ai #aitools #productivity #entrepreneur #startup

competitors.spokenHook: Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix so in thropic the creators of Claude advise that after 20 or 30 messages with Claude you start a brand new conversation but the problem is when you transfer to a new conversation sometimes Claude leaves out important details and so I I created a new skill called the handoff skill that makes sure that Claude captures all the important information and seamlessly transfers it to any new conversation and this handoff skill generates the summary using the exact format below and it's able to capture the core contexts key decisions what's pending and the tone and style of that conversation and then what it does is that it creates this beautiful hand off summary that it can feed directly inside of the next Claude conversation so now whenever you have a really long conversation inside of Claude all you need to do is type in this skill forward slash hand off and it will generate this perfect hand off summary that you can then copy and put into a brand new conversation with Claude to ensure that you start exactly where you left off without losing any context at all so if you want this exact handoff skill and a full guide on the best Claude skills and how to optimize your Claude setup just comment the word Claude and I'll go ahead and send it over to you and as always if you like this sort Of content and you want to stay ahead in the world of AI, make sure that you give me a follow.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Comment “CLAUDE” to get the best Claude optimization and setup guide on the internet. Boris Cherny created Claude Code and he just shared 10 tips on how he uses it. Here are the key takeaways. First, always start with Plan Mode. Do this before any code. Just press Shift+Tab to turn it on. Iterate on your plan. Make it perfect. This stops many errors later. Think of it like drawing a map first. Second, run multiple Claude Code sessions. Use each for a different project part. Then merge them at the end. Let AI do the merge. You finish projects three to five times faster. It’s like having extra hands working at once. Third, the agent makes a mistake? Tell it to update its own claude.md file. Do this every time. It learns. The same mistake won’t happen again. Simple as fixing a recipe note. Fourth, build sub-agents. Use them to automate your work. One agent writes code. Another reviews it. A third deploys it. They all run on their own. Picture a team passing a baton smoothly. The good news? These tips fit any workflow. Because anytime Claude helps with one part, it speeds up the rest: #borischerny #claudeai #claudecode #claudetips #aitipsandtricks

competitors.spokenHook: The creator of Claude Code Boris Cherny just dropped 10 tips on how he uses Claude Code and here are the key takeaways I've personally tried all of them and I'll show you the top five of his tips if you want to get the most out of Claude and stay till the end because the last one is definitely the most impactful and if you like this sort of content and you want to get the most out of your a I experience make sure that you give me a follow tip number one is that he runs multiple Claude agents in parallel inside of his terminal and inside of the Claude desktop app it self and running multiple agents is useful because you can have each agent take care of specific responsibilities like one for coding one for testing one for design one for iteration and one for review tip No. 2 is he uses a shared Claude MD file which is basically a context file that teaches Claude everything about who he is, how he gets work done, his business etc and when you share that with your entire team everybody then has access to that same context so the results that they're gonna create are gonna be similar tip No.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Your AI is only as smart as the context you give it. A solid Context or Claude.md file will make your AI 10x better with personalized outputs. Comment “Claude” and I’ll send you the best Claude guide on the internet. #ai #claude #chatGPT #AIforbusines
competitors.topic: This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately
competitors.onScreenTitle: This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately
competitors.spokenHook: Before you use Claude or any AI system, make sure that you have this one file in place and if you don't have this file, you're gonna find that you always have to repeat yourself with AI and it forgets what you do after every conversation. It's called a context file and it tells AI exactly who you are, what you do, what kind of outputs you want from AI and exactly what you want to achieve in life.

briefMarkdown:
*Generated: May 15, 2026 at 3:38 PM MT*

---

# TikTok Ideas, May 15, 2026, Daily Success Scan refresh

**Strategist read.** Fresh Braydon scrape returned **30 recent posts** and the full competitor Daily Success Scan returned **50 competitor posts across all 10 handles**. This is a stronger read than the earlier degraded scan today. The account is still telling the same story: concrete business-output demos are beating abstract news. Braydon's strongest recent proof is Claude UGC at **159.2K views / 10,169 saves / 1,982 shares**, Claude plus Blotato at **60.0K / 3,646 saves**, Claude Dreams at **37.9K / 1,181 saves**, and Claude Code plus Codex at **12.4K**. Competitor scan shows a live OpenClaw and agent-team cluster, plus a smaller but useful Claude memory/context-file cluster.

**Recommended posting order:**
1. **Lock slot: Turn one local-business lead into a campaign.** Best fresh wave plus Braydon's local-business credibility.
2. **Secondary: Connect Claude to a visual tool.** Stacks Justyn's 48.3K Higgsfield post with Braydon's 159.2K UGC proof.
3. **Backup: The one context file your AI needs.** Competitor proof is smaller but clean and very teachable.
4. **News slot if needed: Codex from your phone.** Still earned by official X signal from today's early-catch log plus Braydon's 12.4K Codex/Claude Code post.

---

## Idea 1: Turn one local-business lead into a campaign

`🌊🔁 wave-ride + V2/reframe`

- **What it's about:** Show a calm, ethical local-business version of the OpenClaw prospecting wave: find one public lead, identify a real marketing gap, mock up a useful offer, and draft a human outreach message. Keep it positioned as a workflow demo, not a get-rich-quick side hustle.
- **Evidence:** Full competitor scan surfaced Marcin's May 12 OpenClaw + Google Maps contractor outreach post at **15.3K views** with complete hook layers: on-screen title **"Openclaw + Google + Ai = $$$$ / Easy $10K a month"** and spoken hook **"Openclaw plus a clever guy using construction to make serious cash..."**. Riley's older OpenClaw Blender demo is still the largest competitor item returned at **699.9K views**. Braydon's own local-business and visual-output demos are strong: Claude UGC is **159.2K**, Arcads/product-link ad is **18.9K**, and Claude website build is **5.5K**.
- **On-screen title:** "AI can find local leads for you"
- **Spoken hook:** "If you run a local service business, this is how AI can help you find one real lead and turn it into a useful campaign idea without spamming people."
- **Content type:** `tutorial / tool breakdown`
- **Why this should work:** It borrows the OpenClaw wave but makes it Braydon: useful, ethical, local-business-first, and easy for Frank to understand.

---

## Idea 2: Connect Claude to a visual tool

`🌊🔁 wave-ride + V2/reframe`

- **What it's about:** Reframe Claude as the front door for visual creation: give it a product photo, brand context, or campaign goal, then connect it to a visual tool to generate the asset. Use a simple before-and-after demo.
- **Evidence:** Justyn's May 12 Higgsfield MCP + Claude post is at **48.3K views**, **2,166 likes**, **629 shares**, with spoken hook **"Powerful as Claude is, there's one thing it just can't do until now and that's create high quality images and videos."** Braydon's own closest adjacent proof is huge: Claude UGC is **159.2K / 10,169 saves**, Blotato social content is **60.0K**, and Arcads from product link is **18.9K**.
- **On-screen title:** "Claude can now make the visual too"
- **Spoken hook:** "Claude is great at thinking through the campaign, but now you can connect it to tools that actually create the visual asset. Here is the simple version."
- **Content type:** `tool breakdown / tutorial`
- **Why this should work:** The audience has already proven they want Claude plus output demos. This gives the same pattern a fresh tool connection and a clear creative payoff.

---

## Idea 3: The one context file your AI needs

`🌊 wave-ride`

- **What it's about:** Teach the simplest version of a context file: one page that tells AI who you are, what you do, your tone, your offers, your customers, and what good output looks like. Avoid dev language and call it a business briefing sheet.
- **Evidence:** Taki has two current Claude context/memory posts in the scan: **17.6K views / 226 comments** on a Claude handoff skill with on-screen title **"The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill"**, and **17.1K views / 234 shares** on a context file with on-screen title **"This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately"**. Both had spoken hooks extracted. Braydon's own skills/context-adjacent posts are lower-view but save-healthy, so this is a useful secondary, not the lock.
- **On-screen title:** "Give AI this one briefing sheet"
- **Spoken hook:** "Most people blame AI when the output is bad, but they never gave it the basics. Here is the one-page briefing sheet I would give every business owner."
- **Content type:** `quick tip / tutorial`
- **Why this should work:** It turns a technical Claude.md/context-file trend into a plain-English business habit. It also reinforces Braydon's core belief that AI is leadership and communication.

---

## Idea 4: What 300 AI agents actually means

`🌊 wave-ride`

- **What it's about:** Use the Kimi K2.6 / 300 parallel agents headline as a teachable moment. Explain that the point is not "300 robots replacing people". The point is breaking one big job into smaller jobs that can run at the same time.
- **Evidence:** NoCode Joshua's Kimi post returned at **49.0K views** with on-screen title **"China just destroyed every AI 🤯"** and spoken hook **"China just did it again and dropped an AI model that competes with ChatGPT and Claude..."**. This is Frank-borderline if handled as model drama, but strong if Braydon turns it into a delegation lesson.
- **On-screen title:** "300 AI agents sounds crazy. Here is what it means."
- **Spoken hook:** "You do not need 300 AI agents. But this headline points to something every business owner should understand: AI work is moving from one chat to a team of smaller tasks."
- **Content type:** `myth-bust / explainer`
- **Why this should work:** It rides a competitor wave without copying the hype. Braydon can be the calm mentor who translates the trend into a practical leadership lesson.

---

## Idea 5: Codex from your phone

`📰🔁 news + V2/reframe`

- **What it's about:** OpenAI put Codex into the ChatGPT mobile app. Braydon's version should not be developer jargon. Make it about starting, checking, steering, and approving AI work while away from the desk.
- **Evidence:** Today's early-catch log still has strong official X signal from May 14: OpenAI's Codex mobile post at **20,519 likes**, **4,425 bookmarks**, and **2,135 quotes**. Braydon's own adjacent Claude Code + Codex post is at **12.4K views** with spoken hook **"You can now use Claude code and Codex together. Watch this."**
- **On-screen title:** "Codex can now work from your phone"
- **Spoken hook:** "Most people think Codex only matters at a computer, but OpenAI just put it in the ChatGPT mobile app. That means you can check the work and steer it from your phone."
- **Content type:** `news / tool breakdown`
- **Why this should work:** It combines a fresh official product signal with Braydon's active Codex momentum. Keep the demo simple and outcome-based.

## Research Notes

### Braydon performance snapshot

- **Top performer:** Claude UGC at **159.2K views**, **7,596 likes**, **84 comments**, **1,982 shares**, and **10,169 saves**. Cached layers: on-screen title **"Claude can make UGC Videos 🤯🤯🤯"** and spoken hook **"Claude can now make UGC videos. Watch this."**
- **Other strong proof:** Claude plus Blotato at **60.0K / 3,646 saves**, Claude Dreams at **37.9K / 1,181 saves**, product-link ad at **18.9K / 915 saves**, Free Claude Courses at **17.3K / 1,118 saves**, and Claude Code plus Codex at **12.4K**.

### Early-catch opportunities

- **OpenClaw local prospecting:** added to early-catch log as a fresh TikTok receipt. Best used as a Braydon-style local-business workflow, not a side-hustle hype post.

## Run notes and limitations

- TikTok metrics scrape success: Braydon scrape returned **30 items**. Competitor scrape returned **50 items across all 10 handles**: `brandnat`, `digitalsamaritan`, `justyn.ai`, `marcinteodoru`, `nocode.joshua`, `rileybrown.ai`, `sabrina_ramonov`, `taki.gpt`, `the.rachelwoods`, `tony.aube`.
- Braydon hook coverage for returned items: **30/30 on-screen titles** and **30/30 spoken hooks** available from cache or WebVTT extraction. One new cover OCR was attempted and cached.
- Competitor evidence coverage for returned items: **24/50 on-screen titles** and **16/50 spoken hooks** available from cache or extraction. For the posts used as evidence above, title and spoken-hook layers were attempted and cached or already available.
- Caption-derived first-words proxies: not used as spoken-hook proof in the final ideas. Items without extracted/cached spoken hooks are treated as caption/performance signals only.
- Known limitation: several competitor results are older posts despite latest sorting, especially Riley and Tony. I used those as pattern proof only and weighted newer May 12 to May 14 posts more heavily for recommendations.
